evals.prompt: Build a responsive gist card component with container queries that adapts its layout based on available space, not viewport width. Include hover micro-interactions.
evals.expected_output: The agent implements container queries for component-level responsiveness, adds hover effects with transform and shadow, uses semantic design tokens, and respects reduced motion preferences.
assertions: The agent uses container-type and container-name CSS properties
The agent implements @container queries for layout changes
The agent includes hover micro-interactions (transform, shadow)
The agent uses CSS custom properties from design tokens
The agent includes @media (prefers-reduced-motion) support

evals.prompt: Implement View Transitions API for smooth page navigation between gist list and gist detail views with fallback for unsupported browsers.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks for document.startViewTransition support, wraps content updates in view transition calls, provides graceful fallback, and adds view-transition-name CSS properties.
assertions: The agent checks if document.startViewTransition exists
The agent wraps DOM updates in startViewTransition()
The agent provides fallback for unsupported browsers
The agent uses view-transition-name CSS property
The agent handles transition lifecycle (finished promise)

evals.prompt: Create a professional bottom sheet component for mobile with drag-to-dismiss, backdrop, focus trap, and ARIA attributes for accessibility.
evals.expected_output: The agent implements a bottom sheet with touch drag handling, backdrop click to close, focus trap for keyboard users, and proper ARIA attributes (role, aria-modal, aria-labelledby).
assertions: The agent implements touch drag gesture handling
The agent includes backdrop with click-to-close
The agent implements focus trap for accessibility
The agent adds ARIA attributes (role=dialog, aria-modal)
The agent handles safe area insets for notched devices

evals.prompt: Build a navigation system that shows bottom tab bar on mobile, rail navigation on tablet, and persistent sidebar on desktop with smooth transitions between breakpoints.
evals.expected_output: The agent implements responsive navigation with mobile bottom nav (72px), tablet rail (72px width), desktop sidebar (240px), using CSS grid for layout, and media queries for breakpoints.
assertions: The agent implements bottom nav for mobile (< 768px)
The agent implements rail nav for tablet (768-1023px)
The agent implements sidebar for desktop (>= 1024px)
The agent uses CSS grid for app shell layout
The agent includes current route visual indicator

evals.prompt: Implement skeleton loading screens with shimmer animation for gist cards while data is loading, respecting reduced motion preferences.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates skeleton placeholders with CSS gradient animation, uses prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable animation, and matches the structure of loaded content.
assertions: The agent implements CSS gradient shimmer animation
The agent respects prefers-reduced-motion media query
The agent matches skeleton structure to content layout
The agent uses design tokens for colors
The agent provides fade transition between skeleton and content

evals.prompt: Create an accessible command palette with keyboard navigation (Cmd+K to open, arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to close), search filtering, and ARIA live regions.
evals.expected_output: The agent implements a command palette with keyboard shortcuts, filtered search results, keyboard navigation between items, and screen reader announcements via aria-live.
assertions: The agent implements Cmd/Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut
The agent supports arrow key navigation
The agent implements search filtering
The agent uses aria-live for result announcements
The agent traps focus while open

evals.prompt: Modernize an existing gist list UI with 2026 best practices: semantic HTML, focus-visible states, reduced motion support, and sophisticated hover effects using CSS custom properties.
evals.expected_output: The agent updates HTML to use semantic elements (article, header, main), adds focus-visible styles, implements reduced motion media query, and creates smooth hover transitions with tokens.
assertions: The agent uses semantic HTML5 elements
The agent implements focus-visible (not just focus)
The agent includes prefers-reduced-motion support
The agent uses CSS custom properties for theming
The agent creates sophisticated hover micro-interactions
